data.title: FOXO Technologies Expands Authorized Share Capital for Growth
data.description:

FOXO Technologies ( (FOXO) ) has provided an update.

On April 15, 2026, FOXO Technologies Inc. obtained stockholder authorization to amend its Certificate of Incorporation and subsequently signed and submitted a Certificate of Amendment to the Delaware Secretary of State. The amendment increases the company’s authorized capital stock to 25,020,000,000 shares, comprising 25,000,000,000 shares of Class A common stock and 20,000,000 shares of preferred stock, each with a par value of $0.0001.

The change, which is scheduled to become effective upon filing on May 3, 2026, was adopted in accordance with Delaware corporate law and significantly expands FOXO’s capacity to issue new equity. This expanded authorization positions the company to access additional capital, pursue financing transactions, or support future corporate actions that rely on a larger pool of available shares, with potential implications for ownership structure and shareholder dilution.

Spark’s Take on FOXO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FOXO is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by persistent losses and continued operating/free-cash-flow burn despite improving revenue and gross margin trends. Corporate events add mixed impact (operational progress but higher dilution risk), while valuation remains constrained by negative earnings and technicals cannot be substantiated from the missing indicator set.

To see Spark’s full report on FOXO stock, click here.

More about FOXO Technologies

FOXO Technologies Inc. is a Delaware-incorporated company that issues Class A common stock and preferred stock, indicating its primary activities center on corporate capital markets and equity financing. The firm’s capital structure is organized under Delaware corporate law, giving it flexibility to raise funds through large-scale share issuance to support its business operations and strategic initiatives.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Allbirds Secures Convertible Financing to Launch AI Compute Pivot
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Allbirds ( (BIRD) ) has issued an update.

On April 14 and 19, 2026, Allbirds entered into and amended a securities purchase agreement with an institutional investor for a senior secured convertible note facility of up to $50 million, with an initial $5.25 million tranche bearing 12% interest and maturing two years after issuance. The notes are convertible into Class A common stock at formula-based prices, are secured by newly acquired electronics assets and equity in a new subsidiary, NewBird AI, LLC, and give the investor significant co-investment rights in future financings.

Proceeds from the facility are earmarked primarily for purchasing high-performance GPU-based server equipment and other “Electronics Assets” through NewBird AI, marking Allbirds’ first step in a strategic pivot into AI compute infrastructure. Using funds from the initial tranche, the company escrowed money to buy NVIDIA Blackwell-based servers and simultaneously signed a three-year, roughly $2.75 million lease of those GPU assets to a QumulusAI subsidiary, inaugurating a new compute leasing business line.

To enable the new financing and lien structure, on April 19, 2026 Allbirds amended its existing credit agreement and entered into a subordination agreement that makes obligations under the convertible facility junior to its bank debt while preserving first-priority security on the electronics assets and NewBird equity. The company has also signed support agreements with certain shareholders and filed a preliminary proxy seeking stockholder approval for issuances exceeding Nasdaq’s 19.99% cap at a special meeting initially targeted for May 18, 2026, which may be postponed.

The convertible facility underpins Allbirds’ amended business plan following its March 29, 2026 asset purchase agreement to sell its loss-making footwear business to an American Exchange Group affiliate, after which the Allbirds brand will continue under the buyer while the public entity rebrands and pursues an electronics infrastructure strategy. Management positions this pivot as a way to tap surging demand for AI compute capacity with capital-light lease and monetization structures, aiming to grow a GPU- and AI-cloud-focused platform while updating investors on new risk factors linked to the shift.

The most recent analyst rating on (BIRD) stock is a Buy with a $14.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Allbirds stock, see the BIRD Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BIRD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BIRD is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by deteriorating financial performance (multi-year revenue contraction, persistent losses, and ongoing cash burn with reduced equity cushion). Technicals add some support due to strong momentum, but the extremely overbought RSI raises pullback risk. Valuation is constrained by losses (negative P/E), and earnings-call commentary remains cautious due to soft revenue/margins and liquidity pressures despite cost and inventory improvements.

To see Spark’s full report on BIRD stock, click here.

More about Allbirds

Allbirds, Inc., a Delaware public benefit corporation listed on Nasdaq as BIRD, historically operated a branded footwear business under the Allbirds name. In March 2026 the company agreed to sell substantially all footwear-related assets, including the Allbirds tradename and intellectual property, to an affiliate of American Exchange Group, which will continue the consumer footwear brand.

Following the closing of this asset sale, the remaining public company plans to adopt a new corporate name, expected to be NewBird AI, and pivot its operations to the computing infrastructure sector. The reoriented business will focus on acquiring and monetizing high-performance graphics processing units and related electronics to provide AI-focused compute capacity, initially through long-term leases and other capital-efficient structures.

Average Trading Volume: 4,919,152

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $148M

For a thorough assessment of BIRD stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: AnaptysBio Completes First Tracks Spin-Off, Refocuses on Royalties
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from AnaptysBio ( (ANAB) ).

On April 20, 2026, AnaptysBio completed the spin-off of First Tracks Biotherapeutics, separating its biotechnology R&D operations from a royalty-focused business that will retain and manage Jemperli and imsidolimab royalty streams and related collaborations with GSK and Vanda. The transaction transferred about $100 million in cash, IP, employees, clinical programs and leases to First Tracks, while AnaptysBio kept royalty-related assets and liabilities, implemented broad mutual indemnities and tax-sharing rules, and put in place a two-year transition services framework.

The separation triggered significant governance and management changes, including the resignations of four directors in April 2026, the move of Chief Medical Officer Paul Lizzul to the same role at First Tracks and a new consulting-based CEO arrangement that keeps Daniel Faga leading AnaptysBio at least through January 15, 2027. AnaptysBio now runs a streamlined, virtual structure aimed at maximizing shareholder returns from its royalty portfolio, while First Tracks assumes full responsibility for advancing rosnilimab, ANB033 and ANB101 and for post-spin employee and benefit obligations.

The spin-off also formalized complex arrangements on asset allocation, shared contracts, IP licensing and access to information designed to minimize operational disruption and clearly allocate risks between the two companies. Both sides agreed to non-solicitation covenants, detailed equity award conversions and ongoing support services so that First Tracks can operate independently while AnaptysBio continues to meet collaboration obligations and maintain its Nasdaq listing under the ANAB ticker.

The most recent analyst rating on (ANAB) stock is a Buy with a $69.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on AnaptysBio stock, see the ANAB Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ANAB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ANAB is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak and volatile financial quality (negative gross profit, historical losses, and past leverage volatility) despite a clear 2025 improvement in revenue and cash flow. Technicals are moderately supportive, corporate actions add a positive catalyst, but valuation remains challenging due to negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on ANAB stock, click here.

More about AnaptysBio

AnaptysBio, Inc., now describing itself as a royalty management company, focuses on managing financial collaborations tied to cancer drug Jemperli with GSK and autoimmune candidate imsidolimab with Vanda Pharmaceuticals. The San Diego-based firm no longer conducts internal biopharma R&D and operates as a virtual company with limited staff, minimal operating expenses and a high projected EBIT margin, while its former drug development assets reside in newly spun-off First Tracks Biotherapeutics.

Average Trading Volume: 525,567

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $1.95B

Find detailed analytics on ANAB stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: USA Rare Earth Announces Merger Agreement with SVRE Holdings
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade USAR

USA Rare Earth ( (USAR) ) has shared an update.

On April 20, 2026, USA Rare Earth, Inc. announced it had entered into a definitive merger agreement dated April 19, 2026 with SVRE Holdings Ltd. through a wholly owned British Virgin Islands merger subsidiary, with Serra Verde Rare Earths Ltd. acting as representative for SVRE’s shareholders. SVRE shareholders will approve the deal by written consent, while USAR plans to seek shareholder approval for issuing common stock as merger consideration via a proxy process with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company emphasized that the merger documentation is intended primarily to allocate risk among the parties and may not reflect all current factual conditions, cautioning investors not to rely solely on the contractual representations and warranties. USAR also outlined extensive risk factors around completing the SVRE transaction and related deals, realizing expected synergies, ramping its Stillwater magnet plant and Round Top project, securing capital and government financing, and managing market, geopolitical and operational uncertainties.

In conjunction with the transaction, USAR will file a preliminary and then definitive proxy statement with the SEC and distribute it to stockholders ahead of the vote, urging investors to review these materials carefully once available. The company noted that certain directors, executives and employees may be deemed participants in the proxy solicitation, with their interests and security holdings to be detailed in SEC filings, while reiterating that the communication does not constitute an offer or solicitation to buy or sell securities.

The most recent analyst rating on (USAR) stock is a Buy with a $30.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on USA Rare Earth stock, see the USAR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on USAR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, USAR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance (severe 2025 revenue collapse, very large losses, and persistent negative free cash flow). Offsetting factors include constructive technicals (price above major moving averages) and a mostly positive strategic narrative from the earnings call and recent corporate actions, though valuation is weakened by ongoing losses and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on USAR stock, click here.

More about USA Rare Earth

USA Rare Earth, Inc. operates in the rare earths and advanced materials sector, focusing on developing an integrated U.S.-based supply chain for rare earth oxide production and neo magnet manufacturing. The company is also advancing the Round Top rare earth deposit and a Stillwater magnet facility, targeting strategic markets tied to electrification, defense and high-tech manufacturing in competition with foreign suppliers.

Average Trading Volume: 19,314,775

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $4B

data.title: Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Announces Forge Nano Merger
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Corporation ( (ATII) ) has shared an update.

On April 20, 2026, Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II entered into an agreement to merge with Forge Nano in a multi-step business combination that will re-domicile the SPAC from the Cayman Islands to Delaware, create a new holding company called Forge Nano Holdings Inc., and value Forge Nano at approximately $1.2 billion in equity consideration, with existing Forge Nano shareholders receiving Pubco stock and potential earn-out of up to 90 million additional shares tied to share price and revenue milestones over five years. The deal, expected to close as early as the third quarter of 2026 subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, is supported by a $100 million PIPE financing featuring 10 million new shares and 15 million warrants plus anti-dilution and reset mechanics, sponsor share contributions to backstop additional financing, and lock-up and registration rights agreements that will concentrate post-closing control with Forge Nano holders and provide substantial upside incentives but also introduce meaningful warrant overhang and potential dilution for public investors.

Conditions to closing include effectiveness of an S-4 registration statement, Hart-Scott-Rodino clearance, Nasdaq listing approval for the new Pubco shares, and the absence of material adverse effects on either party, while either side can terminate the agreement under a range of failure-to-close, breach or regulatory-block triggers before the outside date. Governance at closing will shift to an eight-member board with seven directors designated by Forge Nano and one by the SPAC sponsor, aligning corporate control with the operating target, and a comprehensive package of lock-up restrictions, registration rights and warrant features will shape trading dynamics, capital-raising flexibility and ownership concentration in the newly listed Forge Nano Holdings Inc. following completion of the transaction.

More about Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Corporation

Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II Corporation is a Cayman Islands-incorporated special purpose acquisition company focused on effecting mergers, share exchanges and similar business combinations to take private companies public, with its securities listed on Nasdaq. Through its proposed merger with U.S.-based Forge Nano, Inc., a technology company, Archimedes Tech SPAC Partners II aims to re-domicile in Delaware and transition into an operating holding company under the name Forge Nano Holdings Inc., shifting from a capital-raising shell to an advanced industrial technology platform for public market investors.

Average Trading Volume: 54,233

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $308.9M

Learn more about ATII stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Apple ( (AAPL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 20, 2026, Apple announced that Tim Cook will step down as Chief Executive Officer and become Executive Chair of the Board, effective September 1, 2026, marking a major leadership transition at the company. The Board has appointed John Ternus, currently Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering and a long-time Apple executive since 2001, as CEO and Board member from the same date, while current Chair Art Levinson will assume the role of Lead Independent Director.

Apple highlighted Ternus’s deep experience in hardware engineering, product design, and innovation as key strengths expected to shape its future strategy and operations. The company also disclosed that Ternus has no disclosable family or related-party transactions with Apple’s leadership and will enter into Apple’s standard indemnification agreement for directors and executive officers, signaling a routine but closely watched governance shift for investors and other stakeholders.

Offsetting factors are balance-sheet leverage risk and a premium valuation with a low dividend yield; technicals are supportive but not strongly momentum-driven.

data.title: OneMedNet Faces Nasdaq Non-Compliance Over Minimum Bid Price
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

OneMedNet ( (ONMD) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 14, 2026, OneMedNet disclosed that it had received a notice from Nasdaq staff stating that the company is no longer in compliance with the exchange’s $1.00 minimum bid price requirement, after its common stock traded below that threshold for 30 consecutive business days. Under Nasdaq rules, OneMedNet now has 180 calendar days, until October 12, 2026, to lift its share price to at least $1.00 for ten straight trading days, and could receive a further 180-day extension if it meets other listing standards and commits to a remedial plan.

The company said it will monitor its share price and may pursue options such as a reverse stock split to regain compliance, but cautioned that there is no guarantee it will succeed, and failure to meet the bid price or other Nasdaq requirements could lead to the stock being delisted from the Nasdaq Capital Market, potentially reducing liquidity and access to capital for shareholders and the business.

More about OneMedNet

OneMedNet Corporation operates in the healthcare and medical data technology sector, focusing on solutions that leverage clinical data and imaging for research, diagnostics, or related healthcare applications. The company’s shares are listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market, giving it access to public equity investors and subjecting it to Nasdaq’s continued listing requirements.

Average Trading Volume: 4,119,581

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $46.04M

data.title: NRC Health Adopts New Name and Governance Structure
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from National Research ( (NRC) ).

On April 15, 2026, National Research Corporation formally changed its corporate name to NRC Health through a charter amendment filed in Delaware, aligning its legal identity with the brand it has used since 2016 while keeping its NASDAQ ticker symbol “NRC” unchanged and leaving shareholder rights unaffected. The board simultaneously adopted amended and restated bylaws that, among other technical and administrative updates, declassify the board so all directors stand for annual election starting with the June 23, 2026 annual meeting, set a 12-member board cap, define the chair’s role, and adjust procedures and deadlines for shareholder meetings, nominations, and proposals, with current directors’ term-shortening resignations framed as a procedural step rather than a governance dispute.

These governance changes mark a shift to a more annually accountable board structure, bringing NRC Health’s corporate framework closer to prevailing public-company governance norms and clarifying shareholder engagement processes. For investors and other stakeholders, the combination of the name change and bylaw revisions signals an effort to strengthen brand consistency while enhancing transparency and predictability around board elections and shareholder participation.

Spark’s Take on NRC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NRC is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by deteriorating financial performance (declining revenue and margin compression) and elevated balance-sheet leverage, alongside bearish technical momentum (price below key moving averages and negative MACD). These negatives are only partially offset by positive free cash flow, a ~4% dividend yield, and a constructive corporate update showing record recurring contract value growth.

To see Spark’s full report on NRC stock, click here.

More about National Research

National Research Corporation, which now operates under the name NRC Health, is a NASDAQ-listed company focused on measuring and improving healthcare experiences under the NRC brand it has used since 2016. The business operates within the health services and analytics space, using its NRC identity in the market while maintaining the ticker symbol “NRC” for investors and other market participants.

data.title: CNB Financial to Present at 2026 Shareholder Meeting
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Cnb Financial ( (CCNE) ).

CNB Financial Corporation announced it will deliver a presentation at its 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders on April 21, 2026, with the materials also made available on its website, outlining recent performance metrics and capital position. The presentation highlights 2025 as a transformative year marked by the completion of its largest deal to date, the ESSA Bancorp merger on July 23, 2025, which materially enlarged its loan and deposit base, broadened its branch network and board representation, and underscored a conservative credit culture, solid capital ratios and continued use of non‑GAAP measures to track profitability and efficiency trends.

The company reports post‑merger growth in loans and deposits, evolving deposit mix and loan yields, and stable asset quality supported by its allowance for credit losses framework. While CNB did not repurchase stock in 2025 and retains capacity under its existing program, the enlarged franchise and integrated core and wealth systems position it to pursue mutually sustainable success for clients, employees and investors within a competitive regional banking landscape.

TLGY Acquisition ( (TLGYF) ) has issued an update.

On April 15, 2026, TLGY Acquisition Corporation shareholders approved an amendment to its governing documents to reduce the monthly amount its sponsors must deposit into the trust account to extend the deadline for completing an initial business combination, lowering the cap from $25,000 to $10,000 per month while maintaining a potential six one-month extensions. The meeting had a quorum with about 91% of eligible ordinary shares represented, the extension proposal passed overwhelmingly, a small number of Class A shareholders redeemed their shares, and following the April 16, 2026 filing of the amendment in the Cayman Islands, TLGY’s sponsors deposited $10,000 to extend the termination date of the SPAC by one month from April 29, 2026 to May 29, 2026.

The vote underscores shareholder support for giving TLGY more time and flexibility to complete a business combination on more cost-efficient terms for its sponsors, while only a limited portion of public shareholders opted for redemption, suggesting continued investor willingness to remain in the vehicle as it seeks a target.

More about TLGY Acquisition

TLGY Acquisition Corporation is a Cayman Islands incorporated special purpose acquisition company, or SPAC, formed to identify and consummate an initial business combination. The company holds investors’ capital in a trust account while seeking a suitable target and operates within the broader blank-check and capital markets deal-making ecosystem.

An update from La Rosa Holdings Corp. ( (LRHC) ) is now available.

On April 16, 2026, La Rosa Holdings Corp. announced it would implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its common shares, effective April 20, 2026 at 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time, with the stock continuing to trade on Nasdaq under the LRHC ticker on a split-adjusted basis and a new CUSIP number assigned to the shares. The move will shrink the outstanding share count from about 5.8 million to roughly 583,000 shares, also adjusting options and warrants and rounding up any fractional holdings to whole shares, and is presented as a proactive step to preserve compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price rules and signal the company’s determination to maintain its exchange listing even though it has not yet received a deficiency notice.

data.title: NXP Semiconductors Redeems $750 Million Senior Notes Early
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NXP Semiconductors ( (NXPI) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 20, 2026, NXP Semiconductors announced that subsidiaries NXP B.V., NXP USA Inc. and NXP Funding LLC fully redeemed US$750 million of 3.875% senior notes originally due in June 2026, retiring the debt in line with the terms of the indenture. The move comes as the Netherlands-based chipmaker continues to serve automotive, industrial and IoT, mobile and communications infrastructure customers across more than 30 countries.

Chief financial officer Bill Betz framed the redemption as part of NXP’s disciplined capital allocation strategy, alongside ongoing share buybacks, regular dividends and active balance-sheet management. By eliminating a sizable upcoming maturity, the company is tightening control of its capital structure, which could modestly reduce interest expense and support flexibility for shareholder returns and strategic investments.

The transaction underscores NXP’s effort to maintain a conservative financial profile amid shifting demand cycles in the global semiconductor market. For bondholders, the early redemption crystallizes returns ahead of the original June 2026 maturity, while equity investors may view the move as a signal of confidence in cash generation and balance-sheet strength.

The most recent analyst rating on (NXPI) stock is a Hold with a $235.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NXP Semiconductors stock, see the NXPI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NXPI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NXPI is a Neutral.

The score is led by solid underlying fundamentals—strong margins and robust free-cash-flow generation—tempered by leverage and muted recent growth. Technicals are the biggest near-term drag, with the stock below key moving averages and negative momentum. Valuation is fair-to-slightly-rich (P/E ~27) but supported by a ~2% dividend, while earnings-call guidance was constructive yet balanced by inventory, communications-infrastructure weakness, and near-term cash outlays.

To see Spark’s full report on NXPI stock, click here.

More about NXP Semiconductors

NXP Semiconductors N.V. is a global semiconductor manufacturer serving the automotive, industrial and IoT, mobile, and communications infrastructure markets. The company focuses on system solutions that enhance connectivity, safety, and security, operates in more than 30 countries, and generated $12.27 billion in revenue in 2025.

Positioning itself as a trusted partner for innovative electronics, NXP leverages its “Brighter Together” approach, combining advanced chip technologies with engineering expertise. This strategy supports its role as a key supplier to automakers, industrial customers, and mobile and network equipment makers worldwide.

NXP’s broad geographic footprint and diversified end markets help balance exposure to cyclical swings in individual sectors. Its emphasis on secure, connected solutions aligns with long-term trends in vehicle electrification, factory automation, smart devices, and advanced communications infrastructure.

data.title: RBB Bancorp Details Strong First-Quarter 2026 Financial Performance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rbb Bancorp ( (RBB) ) has provided an update.

RBB Bancorp reported solid first-quarter 2026 results on April 20, with net income rising to $11.3 million, or $0.66 per diluted share, driven by higher net interest income, an expanded net interest margin of 3.15% and improved credit quality as nonperforming assets fell 9% to $48.8 million. The company also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.16 per share payable on May 15, 2026, while maintaining stable asset and deposit levels, underscoring management’s focus on disciplined loan growth, retail deposit gathering and problem asset resolution to support shareholder returns.

Net interest income increased to $30.5 million despite fewer days in the quarter, as lower funding costs and a special Federal Home Loan Bank dividend helped offset modest loan income pressure. Noninterest income improved on higher gains from other real estate owned and recoveries on acquired loans, efficiency strengthened with the ratio improving to 55.41%, and loans and retail deposits both grew year over year, highlighting a more resilient balance sheet heading into the rest of 2026.

On April 21, 2026, RBB Bancorp planned to host a conference call to discuss its results for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 and other company matters. Supporting presentation materials with historical information were made available on the company’s website to provide additional context for investors and analysts reviewing its first-quarter performance.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Radware ( (RDWR) ) just unveiled an update.

Radware announced on April 20, 2026 that it will hold its 2026 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders on May 25, 2026 at its U.S. offices in Mahwah, New Jersey, with April 21, 2026 set as the record date for shareholder participation. The agenda includes electing three Class III directors through 2029, approving equity-based awards and bonus structure changes for the CEO, and reappointing Ernst & Young-affiliated Kost Forer Gabbay & Kasierer as auditors, underscoring ongoing governance, executive compensation oversight and financial reporting continuity for investors.

Shareholders will also review the company’s financial statements and auditors’ report for the year ended December 31, 2025, and may address additional business that properly comes before the meeting. Voting on all proposals will be decided by a simple majority of shares present or represented by proxy, with added approval thresholds for the CEO compensation item under Israeli corporate law, and a quorum will require holders of at least 35% of voting power, highlighting the company’s adherence to both Nasdaq and Israeli regulatory governance standards.

The most recent analyst rating on (RDWR) stock is a Buy with a $35.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Radware stock, see the RDWR Stock Forecast page.
